phoneticUs: 藢tr忙nspl忙n藞te瑟蕛n; 藢tr忙nzpl忙n藞te瑟蕛n
examples: Professor Bruce Lahn of the University of Chicago predicts that humans won't have to donate organs for transplantation.
The terrible constraint on organ transplantation is that every life extended depends on the death of someone young enough and healthy enough to have organs worth transplanting.
Late staged pneumonoconiosis is one of the best indication for isolated lung transplantation.
Over the past decade, there have been many advances in the area of face transplantation.
SangStat (nasdaq: SANG - news - people ) makes drugs used in transplantation.
